tasks.subject: Task 0: HTML extractor helpers — pure functions + RED-GREEN tests
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Land `_extract_sha256` and `_extract_filename` as pure functions over a string with full unit coverage. No `CivArchiveSource` class yet; no fetch; no registry. **Files:** - Create: `src/kinoforge/sources/civarchive/__init__.py` (helpers + module docstring + `_REF_RE` only; `CivArchiveSource` placeholder added in Task 3) - Create: `tests/sources/civarchive/__init__.py` (empty file so pytest discovers) - Create: `tests/sources/test_civarchive.py` (extractor unit tests only) **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] `_extract_sha256(html)` returns the 64-char lowercase hex when `` appears. - [ ] `_extract_sha256(html)` raises `KinoforgeError` with the literal phrase "parser needs maintenance" when no `/sha256/` href is present. - [ ] `_extract_sha256` returns the **first** match when multiple `/sha256/...` hrefs are present (v1 contract). - [ ] `_extract_filename(html)` returns the filename text inside `

NAME.EXT

` for ext in `{safetensors, ckpt, pt, bin, gguf}`. - [ ] `_extract_filename(html)` raises `KinoforgeError` with the literal phrase "parser needs maintenance" when no matching `

` is present. - [ ] `_extract_filename(html)` ignores `.safetensors` text inside `` tags (HF-mirror anchor text); anchored on `

` specifically. - [ ] `_REF_RE` matches `civarchive:111`, `civarchive:111@222`; does NOT match `civitai:111`, `civarchive:abc`, `hf:org/repo`. **Verify:** `pixi run pytest tests/sources/test_civarchive.py -v` ```json:metadata {"files": ["src/kinoforge/sources/civarchive/__init__.py", "tests/sources/civarchive/__init__.py", "tests/sources/test_civarchive.py"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run pytest tests/sources/test_civarchive.py -v", "acceptanceCriteria": ["_extract_sha256 returns hex from /sha256/ href", "_extract_sha256 raises KinoforgeError with 'parser needs maintenance' when missing", "_extract_sha256 returns first match", "_extract_filename returns h4-anchored filename for safetensors/ckpt/pt/bin/gguf", "_extract_filename raises with 'parser needs maintenance' when missing", "_extract_filename ignores HF-anchor-text safetensors (h4-only anchor)", "_REF_RE matches civarchive refs, rejects others"], "modelTier": "mechanical"} ```

tasks.subject: Task 1: Pin HTML fixture from live civarchive page
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Capture the live HTML for `civarchive:2197303@2474081`, scrub nothing, commit as a binary-stable fixture for replay tests in Task 3.

**Files:**
- Create: `tests/sources/civarchive/fixtures/version_2474081.html`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] File exists at the exact path above.
- [ ] File contains the literal hex `67cf1c234f8930472437c3fb9f940d1e05c95261a749c75956831b4ee25fba4d` somewhere in the body.
- [ ] File contains the literal substring `wan2.2_t2v_arcanestyle_high.safetensors` inside an `<h4>` wrapper.
- [ ] File is < 500 KB (pre-commit `check-added-large-files` limit).

**Verify:** `grep -l "67cf1c234f8930472437c3fb9f940d1e05c95261a749c75956831b4ee25fba4d" tests/sources/civarchive/fixtures/version_2474081.html` returns the path; `wc -c < tests/sources/civarchive/fixtures/version_2474081.html` < 500000.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["tests/sources/civarchive/fixtures/version_2474081.html"], "verifyCommand": "grep -q '67cf1c234f8930472437c3fb9f940d1e05c95261a749c75956831b4ee25fba4d' tests/sources/civarchive/fixtures/version_2474081.html && grep -q 'wan2.2_t2v_arcanestyle_high.safetensors' tests/sources/civarchive/fixtures/version_2474081.html && test $(wc -c < tests/sources/civarchive/fixtures/version_2474081.html) -lt 500000", "acceptanceCriteria": ["fixture file exists", "contains the pinned sha256 hex", "contains the canonical filename inside h4", "file size under 500KB"], "modelTier": "mechanical"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: HTTP fetch transport — `_urllib_fetch_html`
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Land the stdlib HTML-fetch transport mirroring `_urllib_fetch_json`: Cloudflare-friendly UA, `HTTPError 401 → AuthError`, anything else → `KinoforgeError`. Inject via constructor in Task 3.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/kinoforge/sources/civarchive/__init__.py` (add transport + `FetchHTMLCallable` type alias)
- Modify: `tests/sources/test_civarchive.py` (add transport-level tests using a stub urlopen)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `_urllib_fetch_html(url, headers)` returns the UTF-8-decoded body string on HTTP 200.
- [ ] Adds `User-Agent: kinoforge-smoke/0.1` to the request.
- [ ] Raises `AuthError` with the URL on HTTP 401.
- [ ] Raises `KinoforgeError` with the URL on any other HTTP error (404, 5xx).
- [ ] Caller-supplied `headers` are merged with the UA (caller's `User-Agent` wins; mirror civitai's `{"User-Agent": "...", **headers}` order).
- [ ] `FetchHTMLCallable` type alias is exported.

**Verify:** `pixi run pytest tests/sources/test_civarchive.py -v -k "fetch"`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/kinoforge/sources/civarchive/__init__.py", "tests/sources/test_civarchive.py"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run pytest tests/sources/test_civarchive.py -v -k 'fetch'", "acceptanceCriteria": ["200 OK returns UTF-8 decoded body", "User-Agent kinoforge-smoke/0.1 attached", "401 raises AuthError with URL", "Non-401 HTTP error raises KinoforgeError with URL", "Caller headers merge into request", "FetchHTMLCallable type exported"], "modelTier": "mechanical"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 3: CivArchiveSource class — handles + resolve with fixture replay
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Land the `CivArchiveSource` class so refs route from the registry to its `resolve()`, building one `Artifact` from the pinned HTML fixture. Bare-ref + invalid-ref + auth-header behaviour all locked in.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/kinoforge/sources/civarchive/__init__.py` (add `CivArchiveSource`; do NOT add the self-register call yet — that lands in Task 4)
- Modify: `tests/sources/test_civarchive.py` (add Source-level tests)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `CivArchiveSource.scheme == "civarchive"`.
- [ ] `handles("civarchive:111@222")` and `handles("civarchive:111")` both True; `handles("civitai:111")` and `handles("civarchive:abc")` False.
- [ ] `resolve("civarchive:111", creds)` raises `KinoforgeError` whose message contains "requires @<versionId>"; spy fetch is NEVER called.
- [ ] `resolve("garbage", creds)` raises `ValueError` whose message contains "Not a valid civarchive ref".
- [ ] `resolve("civarchive:2197303@2474081", creds)` against the pinned fixture returns exactly `[Artifact(filename='wan2.2_t2v_arcanestyle_high.safetensors', url='https://civarchive.com/api/download/models/2474081', size=None, sha256='67cf1c234f8930472437c3fb9f940d1e05c95261a749c75956831b4ee25fba4d', headers={})]`.
- [ ] With `CIVITAI_TOKEN=foobar` in creds, `Artifact.headers == {"Authorization": "Bearer foobar"}`. Empty creds → `Artifact.headers == {}`.
- [ ] HTML fetch invoked exactly once per resolve, with URL `https://civarchive.com/models/<modelId>?modelVersionId=<vid>` and `headers={}` (NO auth on HTML fetch).
- [ ] Privacy: no error message contains the literal substring `https://civarchive.com`.

**Verify:** `pixi run pytest tests/sources/test_civarchive.py -v`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/kinoforge/sources/civarchive/__init__.py", "tests/sources/test_civarchive.py"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run pytest tests/sources/test_civarchive.py -v", "acceptanceCriteria": ["scheme attribute is civarchive", "handles routes civarchive refs and rejects others", "bare ref raises KinoforgeError pre-HTTP", "garbage ref raises ValueError", "fixture replay yields exact pinned Artifact", "CIVITAI_TOKEN propagates to Artifact.headers when present", "HTML fetch is anonymous (no auth header)", "error messages never leak civarchive URL"], "modelTier": "mechanical"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 4: Self-registration + adapter wire-up
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Wire `CivArchiveSource` into the registry on module import, and add the import line to `_adapters.py` so `source_for_ref("civarchive:N@N")` returns it without an explicit registration call.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/kinoforge/sources/civarchive/__init__.py` (add `registry.register_source(CivArchiveSource())` at module bottom)
- Modify: `src/kinoforge/_adapters.py` (add `import kinoforge.sources.civarchive  # noqa: F401` next to existing civitai/hf imports, preserving alphabetical order)
- Modify: `tests/sources/test_civarchive.py` (add self-registration + adapter-import tests)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `import kinoforge.sources.civarchive` registers the source: `registry.source_for_ref("civarchive:1@2").scheme == "civarchive"`.
- [ ] After `import kinoforge._adapters`, `source_for_ref("civarchive:1@2").handles("civarchive:1@2")` is True.
- [ ] No regression: `pixi run pytest tests/sources/ -v` PASS.

**Verify:** `pixi run pytest tests/sources/ -v`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/kinoforge/sources/civarchive/__init__.py", "src/kinoforge/_adapters.py", "tests/sources/test_civarchive.py"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run pytest tests/sources/ -v", "acceptanceCriteria": ["Source self-registers on module import", "_adapters.py imports civarchive source", "no regression across all source tests (civitai, hf, http, civarchive)"], "modelTier": "mechanical"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 5: Live evidence smoke ($0, anonymous)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** One live HTTP GET against `civarchive.com` to confirm the pinned fixture is faithful to the live page shape, with persistent evidence committed. No file-body download.

**Files:**
- Create: `tests/live/evidence/2026-06-28-civarchive-source/resolve.py`
- Create: `tests/live/evidence/2026-06-28-civarchive-source/evidence.md`
- Create: `tests/live/evidence/2026-06-28-civarchive-source/response_meta.json`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `resolve.py` runs end-to-end with `pixi run python tests/live/evidence/2026-06-28-civarchive-source/resolve.py` (no flag, no env beyond defaults).
- [ ] Script writes `response_meta.json` containing at least `status`, `content_type`, `content_length`, `fetched_at` (local-TZ timestamp).
- [ ] Script writes `evidence.md` with date, ref, fetched URL, resolved Artifact repr, and a `Verdict:` line (`PASS` if live sha256 + filename match the pinned fixture; `FIXTURE-DRIFT` otherwise).
- [ ] Live SHA256 observed equals `67cf1c234f8930472437c3fb9f940d1e05c95261a749c75956831b4ee25fba4d`.
- [ ] Live filename observed equals `wan2.2_t2v_arcanestyle_high.safetensors`.

**Verify:** `pixi run python tests/live/evidence/2026-06-28-civarchive-source/resolve.py` exits 0 and prints `Verdict: PASS`; the three evidence files exist after the run.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["tests/live/evidence/2026-06-28-civarchive-source/resolve.py", "tests/live/evidence/2026-06-28-civarchive-source/evidence.md", "tests/live/evidence/2026-06-28-civarchive-source/response_meta.json"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run python tests/live/evidence/2026-06-28-civarchive-source/resolve.py", "acceptanceCriteria": ["resolve.py runs end-to-end with exit 0", "response_meta.json contains status/content_type/content_length/fetched_at", "evidence.md contains Verdict line with PASS or FIXTURE-DRIFT", "live sha256 matches pinned hash", "live filename matches pinned filename"], "modelTier": "mechanical"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 6: Documentation updates + workstream close
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Replace "not yet implemented" stubs in `docs/warm-reuse.md` with a confirmation that civarchive refs resolve. Close the sub-project B workstream block in `PROGRESS.md`. Drop the top-priority pointer block.

**Files:**
- Modify: `docs/warm-reuse.md` (lines 146–149 area — replace the stub)
- Modify: `PROGRESS.md` (add a SHIPPED block; drop the pointer at lines 45–77; update Active workstream line)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `docs/warm-reuse.md` no longer contains the phrase "civarchive source not yet implemented".
- [ ] `docs/warm-reuse.md` mentions HTML scrape + sha256 integrity for civarchive refs.
- [ ] `PROGRESS.md` has a closed "civarchive source (Sub-project B) SHIPPED" block mirroring the sub-project A SHIPPED block shape.
- [ ] `PROGRESS.md` no longer contains the "Sub-project B — civarchive source module" pointer at the prior lines 45–77.
- [ ] `PROGRESS.md` Active workstream line is updated.

**Verify:** `rg -n 'civarchive source not yet implemented' docs/ PROGRESS.md` returns no matches; `rg -n 'civarchive.*SHIPPED' PROGRESS.md` matches a SHIPPED block.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["docs/warm-reuse.md", "PROGRESS.md"], "verifyCommand": "rg -n 'civarchive source not yet implemented' docs/ PROGRESS.md; rg -n 'civarchive.*SHIPPED' PROGRESS.md", "acceptanceCriteria": ["stale 'not yet implemented' text removed from warm-reuse.md", "warm-reuse.md describes HTML scrape + sha256 integrity", "PROGRESS has SHIPPED block for sub-project B", "old pointer block removed from PROGRESS", "Active workstream line updated"], "modelTier": "mechanical"}
```
